reason: All pass criteria met: average 3.33 >= 3.0, minimum score 3 >= 2, no P0 or P1 defects, no red flags. Enrichment suggestions and P2 fixes are improvements to carry forward, not blocking issues.
p2Defects: P2-1: dsimp not disabled — experienced users can bypass show lesson in L02, L03
P2-2: change has no TacticDoc — available but undocumented tactic synonym
P2-3: L02 missing Branch for bare omega — novice's most likely wrong move uncaught
P2-4: show under-practiced after L02-L03 (only 2 uses in world)
P2-5: contradiction introduced but under-used (Branch only, never main path)
P2-6: Duplicate TheoremDoc for Set.mem_univ in L01 and L07
P2-7: L02 introduces two new burdens (set-builder notation + show)
enrichmentSuggestionsToImplement: 1. Add disjunction membership level — L08 conclusion promises left/right for ∨ predicates but no level teaches it; this is narrative drift (failure taxonomy #10)
2. Name the membership reduction pattern — the world's central proof strategy (unfold, work with predicate, close) deserves an explicit name referenced across conclusions
3. Note iff/set equality connection in L09 conclusion — boss has both directions of an iff but never names it; plants seed for ext and SubsetWorld
4. Show sets work on types other than ℕ — all 9 levels use ℕ; one non-ℕ example would make Set α generality concrete
5. Consider: Finite enumerated sets as disjunctive predicates — bridges informal {1,3,5} to formal predicate definition
6. Consider: Mention Odd as counterpart to Even in L05 conclusion
playtestFixesRequired: 1. Add dsimp to DisabledTactic in all SetWorld levels (P2-1)
2. Add Branch to L02 for bare omega attempt with hint explaining why show is needed (P2-3)
3. Remove duplicate TheoremDoc for Set.mem_univ from L07 (P2-6)
5. Consider: Add unfold and delta to DisabledTactic for completeness
notes: The most notable enrichment finding is that L08's conclusion explicitly advertises using left/right for ∨ predicates, but no level teaches this — a promise the world makes and does not keep. This is not blocking (playtest scored coverage at 3 and did not flag it as P0/P1), but it is the highest-priority improvement for the next round if the world is revisited. The world's core job — teaching sets-as-predicates and membership reduction — is done well.
